 {"id":"bd-139x","title":"Phase 7: Performance Conformance (load time and dispatch latency)","description":"# Phase 7: Performance Conformance (load time and dispatch latency)

## Purpose
Not just correctness -- we need competitive performance. The Rust runtime should be FASTER than the TypeScript runtime, or at worst within 2x.

## Metrics

### Extension Load Time
- Measure: time from loadExtension() call to Extension object available
- Target: Rust <= 2x TypeScript for any extension
- Stretch goal: Rust <= 1x TypeScript (faster than TS)
- Method: both harnesses report load_time_ms in their JSON output

### Event Dispatch Latency
- Measure: time from event fire to handler response received
- Target: Rust < 5ms p99 for all event types
- Method: fire 1000 events per type, measure each

### Memory Usage
- Measure: RSS increase from loading N extensions
- Target: Rust <= 1.5x TypeScript per extension
- Method: measure process RSS before/after loading

### Concurrent Extension Stress
- Load 10+ extensions simultaneously
- Fire events to all extensions concurrently
- Verify no deadlocks, no data races, no memory leaks
- Run for 1 hour continuously

## Implementation
- Load time: already captured by differential runner
- Event dispatch: add timing to event fire/response in both harnesses
- Memory: separate benchmark test with RSS monitoring
- Stress: dedicated stress test binary

## Acceptance Criteria
- Load time benchmarks for all 60 official extensions
- Event dispatch p99 < 5ms verified
- Memory growth < 10MB per extension loaded
- 1-hour stress test passes without degradation","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:24:53.762589183Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T00:39:40.110001790Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T00:39:40.109850227Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-139x","depends_on_id":"bd-3odv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:24:53.773572100Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-13gm","title":"Task: Implement agent lifecycle events (startup, agent_start, agent_end)","description":"# Task: Implement Agent Lifecycle Events

## Objective

Dispatch startup, agent_start, and agent_end events at agent lifecycle boundaries.

## TypeScript Reference

```typescript
// At agent initialization
if (runner.hasHandlers('startup')) {
    await runner.emit({ type: 'startup', version: PI_VERSION, sessionFile });
}

// At start of agent.run()
if (runner.hasHandlers('agent_start')) {
    await runner.emit({ type: 'agent_start', sessionId: session.id });
}

// At end of agent.run() (including errors)
if (runner.hasHandlers('agent_end')) {
    await runner.emit({
        type: 'agent_end',
        sessionId: session.id,
        messages: conversation.messages,
        error: error?.message,
    });
}
```

## Events Covered

### startup Event
- **When**: Agent process initialization
- **Purpose**: One-time setup, version check
- **Data**: version, session_file

### agent_start Event  
- **When**: Before first API call in a run
- **Purpose**: Initialize resources, modify system prompt
- **Data**: session_id

### agent_end Event
- **When**: After agent loop ends (success or error)
- **Purpose**: Cleanup, logging, analytics
- **Data**: session_id, messages, error (if any)

## Implementation

```rust
impl Agent {
    pub async fn run(&mut self) -> Result<AgentResult> {
        let dispatcher = self.extension_manager.as_ref().map(|em| em.event_dispatcher());
        
        // Dispatch agent_start
        if let Some(d) = &dispatcher {
            if d.has_handlers("agent_start") {
                d.dispatch::<()>(ExtensionEvent::AgentStart {
                    session_id: self.session.id().to_string(),
                }).await?;
            }
        }
        
        // Run agent loop
        let result = self.run_loop(&dispatcher).await;
        
        // Dispatch agent_end (even on error)
        if let Some(d) = &dispatcher {
            if d.has_handlers("agent_end") {
                let _ = d.dispatch::<()>(ExtensionEvent::AgentEnd {
                    session_id: self.session.id().to_string(),
                    messages: self.session.get_messages(),
                    error: result.as_ref().err().map(|e| e.to_string()),
                }).await;
            }
        }
        
        result
    }
}
```

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (6 cases)
1. test_startup_fires_on_init
2. test_agent_start_fires_before_loop
3. test_agent_end_fires_after_success
4. test_agent_end_fires_after_error
5. test_agent_end_includes_messages
6. test_agent_end_includes_error_message

### E2E Test Script
Extension tracking full agent lifecycle with JSONL logging.

## Dependencies

- Depends on: bd-jt3k (Wire Hostcall Dispatcher)
- Part of: bd-10vp (Extension Event Hook Dispatch)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] startup dispatched on init
- [ ] agent_start dispatched at run start
- [ ] agent_end dispatched at run end (always)
- [ ] Error included in agent_end when failed
- [ ] 6 unit tests pass
- [ ] E2E test passes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:57:31.639958959Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T04:01:56.958694846Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T04:01:56.958630015Z","close_reason":"Agent lifecycle events (startup, agent_start, agent_end) fully implemented in src/agent.rs. startup hook fires at agent init (line ~2593). AgentStart dispatched before first loop (line ~567). AgentEnd dispatched at all exit paths with error info included. Extension dispatch via dispatch_extension_lifecycle_event is fail-open.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-13gm","depends_on_id":"bd-10vp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:57:31.649182507Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-13gm","depends_on_id":"bd-jt3k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:57:49.116039181Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-13gm","depends_on_id":"bd-tg4w","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:57:48.170234734Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-141p","title":"Create deterministic test environment for both runtimes","description":"# Create deterministic test environment for both runtimes

## Context
For differential testing to work, BOTH runtimes must produce identical outputs for the same inputs. This requires eliminating all sources of non-determinism:
- Timestamps (Date.now(), new Date())
- Random values (Math.random())
- File paths (cwd, home directory)
- Process IDs, hostnames
- Network responses (must be mocked)
- Filesystem state (must be sandboxed)

## What To Do
1. For the TS runtime: create wrapper that patches Date, Math.random, process.cwd, etc.
2. For the Rust runtime: ensure QuickJS globals are patched similarly
3. Both runtimes must use identical mock values for:
   - Current time: fixed epoch (e.g., 1700000000000)
   - Random seed: fixed (e.g., always returns 0.5)
   - CWD: /tmp/ext-conformance-test
   - Home: /tmp/ext-conformance-home
4. Document all patched globals

## Why This Matters
Without deterministic environments, outputs will differ due to timestamps alone. Every comparison would be noisy with false positives. Determinism is a PREREQUISITE for meaningful differential testing.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Both runtimes produce byte-identical JSON for a simple extension that uses Date.now()
- Both runtimes produce byte-identical JSON for an extension that uses Math.random()
- CWD and home dir paths are normalized in both outputs","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:16:59.950761840Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T17:48:21.002009680Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T17:48:21.001936804Z","close_reason":"Completed deterministic globals for TS+Rust harnesses and added deterministic diff test","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-141p","depends_on_id":"bd-1v10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:16:59.975032304Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":406,"issue_id":"bd-141p","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Fixed compilation error: Changed double quotes to single quotes in JS string literals inside PI_BRIDGE_JS raw string (lines 4341, 4368-4371). The r"..." raw string delimiter requires single quotes inside JS code. Build now passes and all 60 official conformance tests pass.","created_at":"2026-02-05T17:43:58Z"},{"id":407,"issue_id":"bd-141p","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Deterministic test infrastructure verified: diff_deterministic_globals test passes. Both TS and Rust runtimes produce identical outputs for Date.now(), Math.random(), process.cwd(), and process.env.HOME when PI_DETERMINISTIC_* env vars are set. The fixture extension tests/fixtures/determinism_extension.ts registers a tool with values embedded in name/description for easy verification.","created_at":"2026-02-05T17:45:19Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1q1p","title":"Fix: --theme validation should happen before --version early exit","description":"Bug: when running `pi --theme nonexistent.json --version`, the --version flag causes an early return before theme validation, so invalid themes are silently ignored.

## Expected Behavior
Invalid --theme should error even when combined with --version.

## Current Behavior (before fix)
--version short-circuits on line 83-86 of main.rs, theme validation is never reached.

## Fix Applied
- Moved early theme validation for path-like specs before --version check
- Made `looks_like_theme_path` public in theme.rs
- Named themes (without .json, /, ~) still validated later after resource loading

## Status
Fix is complete but codebase has 56 unrelated compile errors from other agents' work. Fix will pass tests once those are resolved.

## Test

 {"id":"bd-1qk8","title":"pi_agent_rust: migrate sibling deps from path= to version-only + local patch workflow","description":"# Goal
Make `pi_agent_rust` buildable without a sibling checkout by removing `path = ../...` dependencies once the sibling crates are published.

# Current State
`Cargo.toml` uses `version = ...` + `path = ../...` for:
- asupersync
- rich_rust
- charmed_* crates
- sqlmodel_* crates

This is perfect for local dev, but prevents a “pure ecosystem” build.

# Plan
1) Convert dependencies to version-only (no `path =`).
2) Add a documented local-dev override strategy:
   - Preferred: `[patch.crates-io]` entries pointing to sibling paths for developers.
   - Alternative: a `cargo` config snippet or `justfile` (only if patch is insufficient).
3) Add CI proof:
   - A job that builds `pi_agent_rust` with patches disabled (or in a fresh checkout without sibling repos) to ensure we truly depend on published crates.

# Acceptance
- `cargo build` succeeds in an environment with no sibling repos present.
- Local-dev still works with documented patches.
- No behavior  {"id":"bd-1r2f","title":"Task: Implement Session hostcall dispatch","description":"# Task: Implement Session Hostcall Dispatch

## Objective

Implement the Session hostcall handler that routes pi.session.* calls from JS to the Rust SessionContext.

## Background

Extensions call pi.session methods which become Session hostcalls. The dispatch handler must:
1. Receive the hostcall from JS
2. Route to appropriate SessionContext method
3. Serialize result back to JS

## Hostcall Protocol

### Request Format
```json
{
    "type": "session",
    "method": "getMessages" | "getLastNMessages" | "appendMessage" | "fork",
    "args": { ... }
}
```

### Methods

1. **getMessages**: No args, returns message array
2. **getLastNMessages**: { n: number }, returns message array
3. **appendMessage**: { message: Message }, returns void
4. **fork**: { entryId: string }, returns session ID
5. **getId**: No args, returns session ID string
6. **getCwd**: No args, returns cwd string
7. **getStartedAt**: No args, returns ISO timestamp

## Implementation

### Dispatch Handler

```rust
impl HostcallDispatcher {
    async fn dispatch_session(
        &self,
        method: &str,
        args: serde_json::Value,
    ) -> Result<HostcallResponse> {
        let session = self.session_context.as_ref()
            .ok_or_else(|| anyhow\!("No session context bound"))?;
        
        match method {
            "getId" => {
                let session = session.read().await;
                Ok(HostcallResponse::success(json\!(session.id())))
            }
            
            "getCwd" => {
                let session = session.read().await;
                Ok(HostcallResponse::success(json\!(session.cwd().to_string_lossy())))
            }
            
            "getStartedAt" => {
                let session = session.read().await;
                Ok(HostcallResponse::success(json\!(session.started_at().to_rfc3339())))
            }
            
            "getMessages" => {
                let session = session.read().await;
                let messages = session.get_messages();
                Ok(HostcallResponse::success(json\!(messages)))
            }
            
            "getLastNMessages" => {
                let n = args.get("n")
                    .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())
                    .ok_or_else(|| anyhow\!("Missing 'n' parameter"))? as usize;
                
                let session = session.read().await;
                let messages = session.get_last_n_messages(n);
                Ok(HostcallResponse::success(json\!(messages)))
            }
            
            "appendMessage" => {
                let message: Message = serde_json::from_value(
                    args.get("message").cloned()
                        .ok_or_else(|| anyhow\!("Missing 'message' parameter"))?
                )?;
                
                let mut session = session.write().await;
                session.append_message(message)?;
                Ok(HostcallResponse::success(json\!(null)))
            }
            
            "fork" => {
                let entry_id = args.get("entryId")
                    .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
                    .ok_or_else(|| anyhow\!("Missing 'entryId' parameter"))?;
                
                let session = session.read().await;
                let new_id = session.fork(entry_id).await?;
                Ok(HostcallResponse::success(json\!(new_id)))
            }
            
            _ => Err(anyhow\!("Unknown session method: {}", method)),
        }
    }
}
```

### JS Bridge (pi.session)

Update PI_BRIDGE_JS to expose session methods:

```javascript
pi.session = {
    get id() {
        return __pi_hostcall_sync({ type: 'session', method: 'getId', args: {} });
    },
    
    get cwd() {
        return __pi_hostcall_sync({ type: 'session', method: 'getCwd', args: {} });
    },
    
    get startedAt() {
        const iso = __pi_hostcall_sync({ type: 'session', method: 'getStartedAt', args: {} });
        return new Date(iso);
    },
    
    getMessages() {
        return __pi_hostcall_sync({ type: 'session', method: 'getMessages', args: {} });
    },
    
    getLastNMessages(n) {
        return __pi_hostcall_sync({ type: 'session', method: 'getLastNMessages', args: { n } });
    },
    
    appendMessage(message) {
        return __pi_hostcall_sync({ type: 'session', method: 'appendMessage', args: { message } });
    },
    
    async fork(entryId) {
        return await __pi_hostcall({ type: 'session', method: 'fork', args: { entryId } });
    },
};
```

### Binding Session to Runtime

In agent initialization:

```rust
// Bind session to extension runtime
let session_context = create_session_context(session);
runtime.bind_session(session_context);

// Later, session hostcalls can access it
```

## Testing

1. Unit test: getId returns session ID
2. Unit test: getCwd returns working directory
3. Unit test: getMessages returns history
4. Unit test: getLastNMessages returns subset
5. Unit test: appendMessage adds to history
6. Unit test: fork creates new session
7. Integration test: Full flow from JS to Rust

## Dependencies

- Depends on: bd-2i56 (Session implements SessionContext)
- Depends on: bd-2d99 (HostcallDispatcher)
- Part of: bd-3o8s (Session Context Binding feature)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All session hostcall methods dispatched
- [ ] Session bound to runtime correctly
- [ ] Properties (id, cwd, startedAt) work
- [ ] Methods (getMessages, appendMessage, fork) work
- [ ] Unit tests pass","status":"tombstone","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T20:05:17.848075490Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T21:10:05.366015971Z","source_repo":".","deleted_at":"2026-02-04T21:10:05.366010511Z","deleted_by":"ubuntu","delete_reason":"Merged into existing beads (bd-1tyz, bd-1u4t)","original_type":"task","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1rfa","title":"Editor: Shift+Enter newline, Enter submit (legacy multiline)","description":"# Goal
Match legacy multi-line behavior:
- **Shift+Enter** inserts a newline.
- **Enter** submits.
- **Windows Terminal**: **Ctrl+Enter** inserts newline.

# Current Gap
`src/interactive.rs` currently uses different semantics (Alt+Enter mentioned for multi-line mode).

# Implementation Notes
- Ensure this interacts correctly with message queue semantics:
  - When busy, Enter should enqueue steering rather than submit; newline insertion must still be available.
- Preserve standard text editing behavior (cursor movement, deletion).

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Shift+Enter (and Ctrl+Enter on Windows) inserts newline.
- [ ] Enter submits when idle.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

 {"id":"bd-1rgx","title":"Design capability prompt UX spec — information architecture and interaction model","description":"# Goal
Design the complete UX specification for capability prompts — what the user sees, what information is displayed, what actions are available, and how decisions flow through the system.

# Background
When an extension requests a sensitive capability (e.g., filesystem write, shell exec, HTTP request), the runtime needs to ask the user for permission. This task designs WHAT that interaction looks like before any code is written. Getting the UX right is critical because:
- Too much information overwhelms (users click "allow all" and defeat the purpose)
- Too little information prevents informed consent
- The prompt frequency matters — showing a dialog for every fs.readFile call is unusable

# Deliverables
1. Prompt content specification:
   - Extension identity: name, version, publisher
   - Capability requested: human-readable description (not raw enum)
   - Scope: what specifically (file path, URL, command)
   - Risk level: visual indicator (green/yellow/red)
   - Decision options: Allow Once / Allow Always / Deny / Deny Always

2. Grouping/batching rules:
   - Batch multiple requests from same capability class (e.g., 5 file reads → single prompt)
   - Never batch across risk levels (dont mix filesystem with shell exec)
   - Allow "Allow all for this session" as a convenience option

3. Decision persistence model:
   - Keyed by (extension_id, capability_type, scope_pattern)
   - Example: ("auto-commit-extension", "exec", "git *") → AllowAlways
   - Scope patterns support globs for paths and commands

4. RPC mode equivalent:
   - JSON-RPC notification format for prompts
   - Response format for decisions
   - Timeout behavior (deny after 30s with no response)

5. Mockup/wireframe for TUI widget (ASCII art acceptable)

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Written spec document in docs/capability-prompts.md
- [ ] Covers all 5 hostcall types: tool, exec, http, session, ui
- [ ] Includes risk classification rubric
- [ ] Includes batching algorithm pseudocode
- [ ] Includes RPC JSON schema
- [ ] Reviewed against EXTENSIONS.md spec for consistency","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"IvoryIsland","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:36:06.187098597Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:58:37.884885847Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T07:20:34.219000544Z","close_reason":"Completed spec doc in docs/capability-prompts.md","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extensions","security","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rgx","depends_on_id":"bd-2sra","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:20:55Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":517,"issue_id":"bd-1rgx","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"DESIGN REFINEMENT (from review):

The original spec has 4 decision options: Allow Once / Allow Always / Deny / Deny Always.
After review, we need a 5th: "Allow for this session" — a middle ground between "once" and "always".

Rationale: Users commonly want to approve an extension for the current session without persisting the decision permanently. "Allow Once" is too narrow (re-prompts on every call), "Allow Always" is too broad (persists forever). "Allow for session" is the sweet spot for exploratory use.

Updated decision options:
  [Allow Once] [Allow Session] [Allow Always] [Deny] [Deny Always]

"Allow Session" decisions are stored in-memory only (cleared when session ends).

Make sure the UX spec includes this 5th option with clear visual distinction from the persistent options.","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:58:37Z"},{"id":506,"issue_id":"bd-1rgx","author":"IvoryIsland","text":"Drafted full UX spec in docs/capability-prompts.md (risk rubric, batching rules + pseudocode, TUI wireframe, RPC event/command schemas) aligned with EXTENSIONS.md capability taxonomy/policy.","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:20:28Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-22k8","title":"Release: add CHANGELOG.md (generated from closed beads)","description":"# Goal
Add a first-class `CHANGELOG.md` to the repo and define how it is generated/maintained.

# Background
This repo already tracks work granularly via beads. `br changelog` can generate a changelog from closed issues, but we need a stable, user-facing file committed to git.

# Requirements
- `CHANGELOG.md` must be readable without beads context.
- Entries should be grouped by type (feature/task/bug/chore) and/or by release version.
- The file should be regenerated in a deterministic way (same input → same output), or at least documented if manual edits are expected.

# Proposed Workflow
1) Run `br changelog` and capture output.
2) Post-process minimally (if needed): add headings, link issue IDs, remove noise.
3) Commit `CHANGELOG.md` updates as part of the release cut.

# Automation (optional but recommended)
- Add a CI job or a `just`/`make`-style documented command that regenerates changelog output.
- If we publish GitHub Releases, use the same content for release notes.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `CHANGELOG.md` exists at repo root.
- [ ] `br changelog` output mapping is documented (in `docs/releasing.md`).
- [ ] A human can update the changelog during a release with one copy/paste step.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

 {"id":"bd-22p","title":"Workstream: Theme System Implementation","description":"# Workstream: Theme System Implementation

## Goal
Implement theme loading and switching for the TUI, matching Pi Agent's theme system.

## Background
The TypeScript Pi Agent supports themes that control:
- Color palette (foreground, background, accent colors)
- Syntax highlighting colors
- Status line appearance
- Thinking block styling

Themes are stored as JSON files in:
- ~/.pi/agent/themes/ (global)
- .pi/themes/ (project)
- Package themes (installed via pi install)

## Scope

### In Scope
1. Theme file format definition (JSON schema)
2. Theme discovery from standard locations
3. Theme application to TUI components
4. Theme switching via /theme command
5. Default themes (dark, light, solarized)

### Out of Scope
1. Theme creation wizard
2. Real-time theme editing
3. Complex inheritance chains

## Theme Format
```json
{
  "name": "ocean-dark",
  "version": "1.0",
  "colors": {
    "foreground": "#d4d4d4",
    "background": "#1e1e1e",
    "accent": "#007acc",
    "success": "#4ec9b0",
    "warning": "#ce9178",
    "error": "#f44747",
    "muted": "#6a6a6a"
  },
  "syntax": {
    "keyword": "#569cd6",
    "string": "#ce9178",
    "number": "#b5cea8",
    "comment": "#6a9955",
    "function": "#dcdcaa"
  },
  "ui": {
    "border": "#3c3c3c",
    "selection": "#264f78",
    "cursor": "#aeafad"
  }
}
```

## Implementation Phases

### Phase 1: Theme Loading
- Parse theme JSON files
- Discover themes from standard locations
- Validate theme format
- Provide default themes

### Phase 2: Theme Application
- Apply colors to lipgloss styles
- Wire syntax colors to glamour
- Update all TUI components

### Phase 3: Theme Switching
- /theme command to list/switch
- Persist theme preference in settings
- Hot-reload on theme file change (optional)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Theme files parsed correctly
- [ ] Default themes work
- [ ] /theme command works
- [ ] TUI renders with theme colors
- [ ] Settings remembers theme preference

## Files to Create
- src/theme.rs (theme loading/application)
- themes/dark.json (default dark theme)
- themes/light.json (default light theme)

## Files to Modify
- src/interactive.rs (apply theme to TUI)
- src/tui.rs (apply theme to console output)
- src/config.rs (theme preference setting)","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] All child/dependent beads include unit tests + E2E scripts with JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9 (or N/A with rationale) and are closed
[ ] Unit/integration tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this feature area
[ ] E2E scripts validate user-facing workflows; logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Performance/UX budgets or evidence updated where applicable; regressions are detectable
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:39:16.349318511Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T00:07:13.161349662Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T00:07:13.161225581Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-22p","depends_on_id":"bd-2qk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-22r2","title":"Conformance: Third-party GitHub and agent extensions (30 exts)","description":"# Conformance: Third-party GitHub and agent extensions (30 exts)

aliou-pi-extensions (59 entry points!), ben-vargas-pi-packages, charles-cooper-pi-extensions, cv-pi-ssh-remote, graffioh-pi-screenshots-picker, graffioh-pi-super-curl, jyaunches-pi-canvas, kcosr-pi-extensions, limouren-agent-things, lsj5031-pi-notification-extension, marckrenn-pi-sub, michalvavra-agents, ogulcancelik-pi-sketch, openclaw-openclaw, pasky-pi-amplike, qualisero-pi-agent-scip, raunovillberg-pi-stuffed, rytswd-direnv, rytswd-questionnaire, rytswd-slow-mode, vtemian-pi-config, w-winter-dot314 (40 entry points!), zenobi-us-pi-dcp

## Multi-Extension Collections
Several repos contain MANY extensions:
- aliou-pi-extensions: 59 individual extension entry points
- w-winter-dot314: 40 individual extension entry points
- These need per-entry-point testing, not per-directory

## Agent Collections
- agents-mikeastock: 6 extension dirs
- agents-wshobson: agent-related extensions

## Acceptance Criteria
- All third-party extensions attempted (including sub-extensions within collections)
- 75%+ pass rate (lower bar because these are less curated)
- Multi-extension collections properly enumerated and tested individually","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:24:25.099332737Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T21:25:23.820747670Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T21:15:41.004863244Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-22r2","depends_on_id":"bd-21dv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:24:25.112754832Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-22r2","depends_on_id":"bd-3ay7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:24:25.119040241Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-22r2","depends_on_id":"bd-7hgy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:24:25.106187768Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":435,"issue_id":"bd-22r2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Third-party conformance at 78.3% (18/23). @@ -486,15 +486,16 @@ bv --robot-insights | jq '.Cycles'                         # Circular deps (must
 
 ## UBS — Ultimate Bug Scanner
 
-**Golden Rule:** `ubs <changed-files>` before every commit. Exit 0 = safe. Exit >0 = fix & re-run.
+**Golden Rule:** `ubs --staged --only=rust .` before every commit. Exit 0 = safe. Exit >0 = fix & re-run.
 
 ### Commands
 
 ```bash
-ubs file.rs file2.rs                    # Specific files (< 1s) — USE THIS
-ubs $(git diff --name-only --cached)    # Staged files — before commit
-ubs --only=rust,toml src/               # Language filter (3-5x faster)
+ubs --staged --only=rust .              # Staged Rust changes — USE THIS
+ubs --diff --only=rust .                # Unstaged+staged Rust changes vs HEAD
+ubs --only=rust,toml .                  # Language-filtered project scan
 ubs --ci --fail-on-warning .            # CI mode — before PR
+timeout 60s ubs --staged --only=rust .  # Bounded pre-commit run if UBS stalls
 ubs .                                   # Whole project (ignores target/, Cargo.lock)
 ```
 
@@ -515,7 +516,7 @@ Parse: `file:line:col` → location | 💡 → how to fix | Exit 0/1 → pass/fa
 2. Navigate `file:line:col` → view context
 3. Verify real issue (not false positive)
 4. Fix root cause (not symptom)
-5. Re-run `ubs <file>` → exit 0
+5. Re-stage and re-run `ubs --staged --only=rust .` → exit 0
 6. Commit
 
 ### Bug Severity
